title: Cranks But Won't Start
checks: Verify fuel level and listen for fuel pump prime (2–3 sec hum when key to ON)
Check for spark at a plug ( insulated pliers, grounded — engine off between tests)
Scan for immobilizer, security, or crankshaft/camshaft sensor codes
Inspect battery voltage (12.4V+ resting; drops below 10V while cranking indicates weak battery)
commonCauses: No spark (coil, module, or timing sensor)
safetyNotes: Do not spray starting fluid into intake unless manufacturer allows — fire risk
Keep face and hands away from moving belts while cranking

title: Rough Idle
checks: Scan for misfire codes (P0300 series) and pending codes
Inspect vacuum lines and PCV system for leaks
Check air filter and MAF sensor condition
Monitor idle RPM and fuel trims with scan tool at operating temperature
commonCauses: Dirty throttle body or IAC issue
Failed engine mount (vibration, not always a code)
safetyNotes: Keep loose clothing and tools away from belts and fans

title: Check Engine Light On
checks: Read all stored and pending codes with OBD-II scanner
Note if light is steady vs flashing (flashing = active misfire, reduce load immediately)
Record freeze-frame data if available
Clear codes only after repair — note which return after drive cycle
safetyNotes: Flashing CEL indicates catalyst-damaging misfire — avoid hard acceleration

title: Engine Overheating
checks: Stop driving if gauge in red zone — pull over safely
Check coolant level only when engine is cool
Inspect for visible leaks at radiator, hoses, water pump
Verify radiator fan operation at operating temperature (engine off if checking blades by hand)
commonCauses: Failed water pump or radiator fan
safetyNotes: Never remove radiator cap on a hot engine — scalding risk
Do not continue driving with overheating — severe engine damage possible

title: Brake Concerns
checks: Inspect pad thickness through wheel spokes if visible
Check brake fluid level in master cylinder (vehicle level, cap clean)
Note if pedal feels soft, hard, or pulsates
Listen for grinding (metal-on-metal indicates immediate service needed)
commonCauses: Low brake fluid from leak or pad wear
Moisture in brake fluid (soft pedal — professional bleed required)
safetyNotes: Do not attempt brake hydraulic bleeding without proper training and equipment
Grinding brakes reduce stopping ability — limit driving until inspected

title: Engine Stalling
checks: Scan for codes immediately after stall if possible
Check for vacuum leaks and dirty throttle body
Monitor fuel pressure and crankshaft/camshaft sensor data if scan tool supports
Note if stall happens at idle, deceleration, or under load
commonCauses: Idle air control or throttle body carbon
safetyNotes: Stalling in traffic is hazardous — prioritize safe stopping location

title: Exhaust Smoke
checks: Identify color: blue (oil), white sweet smell (coolant), black (rich fuel)
Check oil level and coolant level
Scan for misfire, fuel trim, or catalyst codes
Note if smoke appears at startup only vs continuous
commonCauses: Blue: worn piston rings or valve seals
White: head gasket or coolant intrusion
Black: rich fuel mixture or clogged air filter
safetyNotes: Exhaust contains CO — diagnose outdoors with ventilation
Coolant in combustion chamber is serious — avoid extended driving
